stories: Good evening Sean, it's Saturday evening, the second of May, 2026
Here's one to open with: Eurovision 2026 is heading to Vienna, kicking off on the twelfth of May — just ten days away
Austria won hosting rights after a surprise victory last year, and the competition has been running since 1956
If you've never watched the final, this might be the year to stay up for it
Your calendar is clear for tomorrow — Sunday the third of May is all yours
The long weekend rolls on, with no calls or meetings in sight
On Singapore today: speculation is building around the top job at Singapore Airlines
Industry sources indicate that Vinod Kannan, a senior SIA executive, and Air India's commercial head Nipun Aggarwal are the leading candidates to succeed the current CEO

Good evening Sean, it's Saturday evening, the second of May, 2026
I hope you've had a relaxed first day of the long weekend
Here's a quietly humbling fact to start tonight: Mount Everest isn't actually the tallest mountain on Earth
Mauna Kea, in Hawaii, rises a full kilometre higher than Everest from base to summit â most of it just hidden under the Pacific
A nice reminder that what looks like the obvious leader from the surface alone usually isn't the whole story
Looking ahead to tomorrow â Sunday the third of May â your calendar is clear, so the long weekend rolls on uninterrupted
A genuine day off, with no meetings, no pre-reads, and nothing to log into
Singapore's forecast is warm and humid with afternoon thundery showers, so an early ride or a breakfast walk is probably the best window before the rain rolls in
Good morning Sean, it's Saturday, the second of May, 2026
A nice one to start your weekend: a typical fluffy cumulus cloud weighs around a million tonnes, roughly the same as a hundred and fifty Eiffel Towers
The reason it stays up there is that the warm, moist air carrying it is just slightly less dense than the air around it
So next time one drifts past your window, picture a few thousand elephants quietly riding on a thermal
Your calendar is clear today, so the long weekend rolls on uninterrupted
Closer to home, a couple of Singapore stories worth knowing
And a lighter one: as part of Singapore HeritageFest 2026, organisers are launching a first-of-its-kind theatrical show staged on board a bumboat, celebrating the city's maritime history
A nice excuse to wander down to the river

Good evening Sean, it's Friday evening, the first of May, 2026
Here's a quietly lovely one to start your evening: when Apollo 16 astronaut Charles Duke walked on the moon back in 1972, he left behind a small family photo of himself, his wife, and his two boys
It's still up there, fifty-four years later, sitting in the lunar dust where no wind will ever move it
Your calendar is clear for tomorrow, so Saturday looks fully open
On home turf, Prime Minister Lawrence Wong delivered his May Day Rally today, and the headline line was a sharp one
As AI reshapes whole industries, he said, the government cannot promise to protect every job, but it will commit to protecting every worker
The labour movement also pledged thirty-seven million dollars this year to help workers and their families adapt
Worth listening to in full when you have a moment
